description:
You are acting as the on-call security engineer.  
    A routine credential rotation is scheduled and you must (1) back-up the current token, (2) install a new one, and (3) record a very small performance benchmark of how long that rotation took.

    Starting situation in the container:
        • Directory /home/user/.secrets/ already exists.  
        • Inside it there is a file named /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.txt that currently contains exactly the single line  
          OLD_TOKEN_qwerty  
          (with the trailing newline shown above).

    Your objectives
        1. Move the old token file to the deterministic name  /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.bak  
           – the resulting .bak file must keep the original single-line content unchanged.
        2. Create a brand-new file  /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.txt  that contains exactly the single line  
           NEW_TOKEN_ABC123  
           (again with a trailing newline).
        3. Measure the total wall-clock time, in milliseconds, that the two steps above take.  
           You may take timestamps before and after the rotation logic or use another
           standard GNU/Linux timing mechanism, but you must convert the elapsed time to an integer number of **milliseconds**.
        4. Write a log file named /home/user/credential_rotation_benchmark.log whose **entire** contents are THREE lines in the following fixed format (no extra spaces, no extra blank lines):  

           ROTATION_START_MS=<epoch_ms_start>  
           ROTATION_END_MS=<epoch_ms_end>  
           ROTATION_DURATION_MS=<elapsed_ms>  

           • <epoch_ms_start> and <epoch_ms_end> are Unix time in milliseconds.  
           • <elapsed_ms> is (ROTATION_END_MS − ROTATION_START_MS).  
           • All three values must be strictly numeric and positive.

    Constraints & verification notes
        • The whole rotation (steps 1-2) will usually finish in well under 10 000 ms, so the automated tests will fail if ROTATION_DURATION_MS ≥ 10 000.  
        • After completion, the tests will look for:  
            – existence of /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.bak containing “OLD_TOKEN_qwerty\n”  
            – existence of /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.txt containing “NEW_TOKEN_ABC123\n”  
            – existence of /home/user/credential_rotation_benchmark.log matching the exact three-line
              key=value format described above, with numeric values where  
              ROTATION_START_MS < ROTATION_END_MS and  
              ROTATION_DURATION_MS = ROTATION_END_MS − ROTATION_START_MS.  

    Complexity expectation  
        A knowledgeable engineer can finish this in roughly 3–4 shell commands.
truth:
<!-- STARTING STATE REQUIRED FOR TESTS -->
    File: /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.txt
    Contents (including newline):
    OLD_TOKEN_qwerty\n

    Directory: /home/user/.secrets/  (already exists, writable to the regular user)

    <!-- EXPECTED POST-TASK STATE -->
    1. /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.bak
       Contents: OLD_TOKEN_qwerty\n

    2. /home/user/.secrets/old_api_token.txt
       Contents: NEW_TOKEN_ABC123\n

    3. /home/user/credential_rotation_benchmark.log  
       Exact layout, three lines terminated by newlines:  
           ROTATION_START_MS=<numeric>  
           ROTATION_END_MS=<numeric>  
           ROTATION_DURATION_MS=<numeric>  

       Additional conditions verified by tests:  
           • All three values are integers.  
           • ROTATION_START_MS < ROTATION_END_MS  
           • ROTATION_DURATION_MS = ROTATION_END_MS − ROTATION_START_MS  
           • ROTATION_DURATION_MS < 10000
